steps.title: Find the two clauses
steps.content: The sentence compares two research teams' collection methods. The first complete clause says Hartley's team collected arthropods with sweep nets, and the second complete clause says La Pierre's team gathered them with bug vacuums.

steps.title: Punctuate the example phrase
steps.content: B puts for instance with the second clause instead of the first. C has no needed punctuation. D creates a comma splice between two complete clauses. The parenthetical example phrase needs commas, and the clauses need a semicolon. The answer is A.
